tasks.title: Migration: rename featuredByPlan column to featuredByEntitlement
tasks.description:
Implements OQ-1. Rename `accommodations.featured_by_plan` -> `accommodations.featured_by_entitlement` (TS: `featuredByPlan` -> `featuredByEntitlement`) since the column now reflects plan+addon union, not plan only.

Key files:
- packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/accommodation.dbschema.ts:79 (column def) and lines 141-149 (3 indexes: `accommodations_featuredByPlan_idx`, `accommodations_visibility_featuredByPlan_idx`, `accommodations_destinationId_featuredByPlan_visibility_idx` -> rename to the `...featuredByEntitlement...` equivalents).
- packages/db/src/models/accommodation/accommodation.model.ts:184-191 (`buildAccommodationOrderBy`, the `sql\`(${accommodations.isFeatured} OR ${accommodations.featuredByPlan})\`` disjunction -> update column reference).

Do NOT run `pnpm db:generate` yet in this task — T-002 adds a second schema change (new link table) and runs ONE `db:generate` covering both diffs, producing a single migration file (next number after 0038, i.e. 0039_<slug>.sql). This task only edits the TS schema + model source so the diff is ready.

Acceptance criteria:
- All `featuredByPlan` references in the two files above are renamed to `featuredByEntitlement` (column, index names, sql expression).
- No other file changed in this task (call-site renames happen in T-005/T-008..T-014 once the service-layer function is renamed).
- pnpm typecheck fails loudly at every remaining call site still referencing the old TS property name — this is expected and confirms the rename touches everything; leave those failures for the tasks that own each file.

Testing requirements:
- None yet (covered by T-021/T-022 once primitives exist). Verify visually that the 2 edited files no longer contain `featuredByPlan`.

subtasks.title: Rename column + comment block in accommodation.dbschema.ts

typecheck.details: scoped to packages/db (task touches only this package); repo-wide breakage at other featuredByPlan call-sites is expected and deferred to T-005/T-008..T-014

tests.details: pure structural rename, no new behavior; test coverage deferred to T-021/T-022 once sync primitives exist

tasks.title: Migration: new featured_listing_addon_grants link table + generate
tasks.description:
Implements the OQ-3 scoping mechanism. `billing_addon_purchases` (packages/db/src/schemas/billing/billing_addon_purchase.dbschema.ts) has no accommodation reference and a unique-active-per-(customerId, addonSlug) constraint, so a NEW link table is required to tie one addon purchase to exactly one accommodation, following the `commerce_listing_subscriptions` pattern (packages/db/src/schemas/commerce/commerce_listing_subscription.dbschema.ts) referenced in CLAUDE.md.

Create packages/db/src/schemas/billing/featured_listing_addon_grant.dbschema.ts:
- Table `featured_listing_addon_grants`: `id` uuid PK default random, `purchaseId` uuid NOT NULL references `billingAddonPurchases.id` onDelete cascade, `accommodationId` uuid NOT NULL references `accommodations.id` onDelete cascade, `createdAt`/`updatedAt` timestamps.
- `uniqueIndex` on `purchaseId` (one purchase links to exactly one accommodation).
- `index` on `accommodationId` (resolver lookup path, see T-004).
- No denormalized `status`/`expiresAt` columns — the resolver JOINs to `billingAddonPurchases` for `status = 'active' AND (expiresAt IS NULL OR expiresAt > now())` to avoid duplicated/stale state.
- Export relations (`one` to both `billingAddonPurchases` and `accommodations`) mirroring `commerce_listing_subscriptions`'s relations block.
- Register the new table in the relevant `packages/db/src/schemas/billing/index.ts` (or equivalent) barrel.

This task runs `pnpm db:generate` AFTER T-001's schema edit is in place (blockedBy T-001) so BOTH the column rename and the new table land in ONE migration file (0039_<slug>.sql). Verify no other unrelated drift is swept in.

Acceptance criteria:
- `featured_listing_addon_grants` table exists with the columns/constraints above.
- `pnpm db:generate` produces exactly one clean migration covering the T-001 rename + this new table, no other drift.
- `pnpm db:migrate` applies cleanly on a fresh DB.
- pnpm typecheck green for the new schema file and its barrel export.

Testing requirements:
- Verify both changes via Drizzle Studio or `psql \d accommodations` / `\d featured_listing_addon_grants`.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm db:generate (covers T-001 + T-002) and commit migration 0039
Verify fresh-DB pnpm db:migrate applies cleanly

tests.details: migration-only task, verified via psql schema inspection on worktree_spec_309 (fresh DB clone of hospeda_template): column rename + new table/FKs/indexes/unique constraint all confirmed present

tasks.title: Addon config: add requiresAccommodationTarget flag
tasks.description:
Marks which addons need an accommodation target captured at purchase time (OQ-3). Add an optional `requiresAccommodationTarget?: boolean` field to the `AddonDefinition` type (packages/billing/src/types/addon.types.ts) and set it to `true` on both `VISIBILITY_BOOST_ADDON` and `VISIBILITY_BOOST_30D_ADDON` in packages/billing/src/config/addons.config.ts (lines 7-37). Default is `false`/`undefined` for all other addons (no behavior change for them).

Key files:
- packages/billing/src/types/addon.types.ts (add field to `AddonDefinition`)
- packages/billing/src/config/addons.config.ts:7-37 (set `requiresAccommodationTarget: true` on the two visibility-boost addons)

Acceptance criteria:
- `AddonDefinition` type includes the new optional boolean field.
- Both visibility-boost addons have `requiresAccommodationTarget: true`; no other addon definition changed.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Unit test (packages/billing/test/) asserting `getAddonBySlug('visibility-boost-7d').requiresAccommodationTarget === true` and that an unrelated addon (e.g. an existing non-featured addon) is `false`/`undefined`.

subtasks.title: Set true on both visibility-boost addon definitions
Unit test asserting the flag on both addons

tasks.title: Build shared featured-entitlement resolver helpers
tasks.description:
Implements the union logic G-1 requires (mirrors `loadEntitlements`'s plan UNION customer-entitlement pattern in apps/api/src/middlewares/entitlement.ts:549-558), extracted into ONE reusable module instead of the duplicated `getPlanBySlug(...).entitlements.includes(EntitlementKey.FEATURED_LISTING)` check currently copy-pasted across 8 call-sites.

Create packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/featured-entitlement.resolver.ts exporting:
- `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured({ ownerId }): Promise<boolean>` — resolves the owner's active/trialing/comp subscription (SubscriptionStatusEnum set, matching `featured-by-plan-reconcile.job.ts:94`'s `active|trialing|comp`) and checks `plan.entitlements.includes(EntitlementKey.FEATURED_LISTING)`. This is the single source of truth other tasks (T-005, T-014, T-019) call instead of re-deriving plan status locally.
- `resolveAccommodationHasActiveFeaturedAddon({ accommodationId }): Promise<boolean>` — queries `featured_listing_addon_grants` (T-002) JOIN `billing_addon_purchases` WHERE `accommodationId = ? AND status = 'active' AND (expiresAt IS NULL OR expiresAt > now())`.
- `getOwnerAccommodationIdsWithActiveFeaturedAddon({ ownerId }): Promise<string[]>` — same join, scoped to all of an owner's accommodations, returns the protected id list (consumed by T-005's exclusion logic on revoke).

All three follow RO-RO with full JSDoc; direct Drizzle queries via `getDb()` (no BaseCrudService, consistent with the `accommodation.sync-featured-by-plan.ts` layering precedent).

Acceptance criteria:
- All three functions exported from the new file and the service-core barrel.
- `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured` treats paused as NOT active and comp as active (matches reconcile cron, not the pre-G-5 hook behavior).
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-021.

typecheck.details: scoped to packages/service-core, filtered for the one pre-existing expected error in accommodation.sync-featured-by-plan.ts:118 (T-005's file, still referencing the pre-T-001-rename featuredByPlan property) -- zero errors from the new resolver file

tests.details: per task spec, unit tests covered by T-021

tasks.title: Rename and harden sync primitives with addon-aware exclusion/guard
tasks.description:
Closes H-1's two consequences. Rename packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.sync-featured-by-plan.ts -> accommodation.sync-featured-by-entitlement.ts and change its exports:

- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner({ ownerId, active, db? }): Promise<{ updated: number }>` (renamed from `syncFeaturedByPlan`, same owner-wide bulk shape). When `active === false` (plan-driven revoke), FIRST call `getOwnerAccommodationIdsWithActiveFeaturedAddon({ ownerId })` (T-004) and add `notInArray(accommodations.id, protectedIds)` to the WHERE clause so accommodations with a live addon grant are NOT cleared just because the plan lapsed — this is H-1 consequence (b). When `active === true`, unconditional bulk update (unchanged, addon-protected rows are already true so no harm).
- NEW `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation({ accommodationId, active, db? }): Promise<{ updated: number }>` — single-row target for G-2's addon-purchase/expiry hooks. When `active === false` (addon expiring), FIRST call `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured` (T-004) for that accommodation's owner; if the plan still grants it, return `{ updated: 0 }` (no-op) instead of clearing — this is H-1 consequence (a)'s mirror image for the addon-expiry direction.
- `.returning({ id, slug })` instead of just `{ id }` on both (slug needed by T-017/T-018's revalidation).

Key files:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.sync-featured-by-entitlement.ts (renamed file)
- service-core barrel (update export names)

Acceptance criteria:
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner(ownerId, false)` does NOT clear accommodations present in the addon-protected set.
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation(accommodationId, false)` is a no-op when the owner's plan still grants FEATURED_LISTING.
- Both functions still exclude soft-deleted rows and remain idempotent.
- `.returning()` includes `slug`.
- pnpm typecheck green (expect and fix cascading errors at every old `syncFeaturedByPlan` import — those are the 8 call-sites owned by T-008..T-013).

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-022.

subtasks.title: Rename file + syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner with addon-protected exclusion on revoke
New syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation with plan-still-grants guard on revoke

typecheck.details: packages/service-core scoped typecheck fully clean (zero errors). apps/api scoped typecheck confirms exactly the 8 expected cascading errors at the documented call-sites (apply-scheduled-plan-changes, dunning, finalize-cancelled-subs, featured-by-plan-reconcile x2, qzpay-admin-hooks, payment-logic, subscription-logic) -- deferred to T-008..T-014 per acceptance criteria

New addon-guard behavior + syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation deferred to T-022 per task spec

tasks.title: Checkout: capture and validate accommodationId for target-required addons
tasks.description:
First half of the OQ-3 checkout wiring. In apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts, extend the `PurchaseAddonInput` type (and its schema at apps/api/src/schemas/addon.schema.ts, if the request validates there) with an optional `accommodationId?: string`. In `createAddonCheckout` (line 209 onward), after resolving `addon` (line 214-223): if `addon.requiresAccommodationTarget` (T-003) is true, require `input.accommodationId` to be present (400/VALIDATION_ERROR if missing) and verify it belongs to the requesting customer's owned, non-deleted accommodations (reuse the accommodation service/model ownership lookup — do not hand-roll a query). Reject with 403/`FORBIDDEN` if the accommodation belongs to a different owner. If `requiresAccommodationTarget` is false/undefined, `accommodationId` is ignored (backward compatible for all other addons).

Key files:
- apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts:209 (createAddonCheckout)
- apps/api/src/schemas/addon.schema.ts (if the HTTP-facing schema needs the field)

Acceptance criteria:
- Purchasing `visibility-boost-7d`/`-30d` without `accommodationId` fails validation.
- Purchasing with an `accommodationId` owned by a different user fails with FORBIDDEN.
- Purchasing with a valid own accommodationId succeeds and the value flows to whatever mechanism the checkout module already uses to carry request-time context across the async payment confirmation (investigate the existing pattern used for `promoCode` in this same file — it must survive to `confirmAddonPurchase`).
- Purchasing any OTHER addon (requiresAccommodationTarget unset) is unaffected.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Unit tests in apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.test.ts: missing-accommodationId-for-required-addon (fail), foreign-accommodationId (403), valid-accommodationId (success), unrelated-addon-ignores-field (success, unchanged behavior).

subtasks.title: Identify and reuse the existing checkout-time context-carrying mechanism (cf. promoCode)

tests.details: apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.test.ts 56/56 (5 new: missing-accommodationId VALIDATION_ERROR, foreign-owner FORBIDDEN, soft-deleted NOT_FOUND, valid-own success+metadata, unrelated-addon unaffected); apps/api/test/services/addon.service.test.ts 56/56; packages/schemas addon.schema.test.ts 42/42; packages/seed billingAddons.seed.test.ts 19/19; packages/service-core addon-catalog.* 103/103 — no regressions

tasks.notes: Scope grew beyond the original description: the addon.requiresAccommodationTarget flag added by T-003 only reached the STATIC config catalog (packages/billing ALL_ADDONS). createAddonCheckout reads the DB-backed AddonCatalogService instead, whose mapper never surfaced the field and whose seed never wrote it — so the T-006 guard would never fire in production. Fixed (user-approved) by extending addon-catalog.mapper.ts + billingAddons.seed.ts to read/write metadata.requiresAccommodationTarget, symmetric with durationDays/targetCategories/sortOrder.

tasks.title: Thread accommodationId through confirm flow and write the link row
tasks.description:
Second half of OQ-3 checkout wiring. In `confirmAddonPurchase` (apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts:558) — the point where a `billing_addon_purchases` row is finalized/activated — read back the `accommodationId` carried from T-006's checkout-time context, and when `addon.requiresAccommodationTarget` is true, insert a row into `featured_listing_addon_grants` (T-002) linking the new `purchaseId` to that `accommodationId`. If the accommodationId is unexpectedly missing at confirm time for a target-required addon (should not happen if T-006 validated correctly, but MercadoPago webhooks are async and can race), log a Sentry-visible error and soft-fail the link-write WITHOUT blocking the purchase confirmation itself (the purchase still activates; a follow-up manual reconciliation is out of scope here — leave a TODO comment referencing this task).

Key files:
- apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts:558 (confirmAddonPurchase)

Acceptance criteria:
- A confirmed purchase of a target-required addon produces exactly one `featured_listing_addon_grants` row linking `purchaseId` -> `accommodationId`.
- Confirming any other addon (not target-required) does not touch the new table.
- Missing accommodationId at confirm time never blocks purchase confirmation (soft-fail + Sentry).
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Unit test in apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.test.ts: confirm target-required purchase -> link row exists; confirm non-target addon -> no link row; missing-accommodationId-at-confirm -> purchase still confirms, error logged (mock Sentry.captureException, assert called).

subtasks.title: Insert featured_listing_addon_grants row on confirm for target-required addons
Soft-fail + Sentry capture on unexpected missing accommodationId
Unit tests: link-row created / not created / soft-fail path

typecheck.details: scoped to apps/api (only the 8 pre-existing T-008..T-014 errors remain)

tests.details: apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.test.ts 60/60 (4 new: link row on confirm, no link row for unrelated addon, soft-fail on missing accommodationId, soft-fail on insert throw — captureBillingError asserted); addon.service.test.ts + inv1-cache-invalidation.guard.test.ts 74/74 — no regressions

tasks.title: G-1/G-5: wire subscription-logic.ts to the union resolver
tasks.description:
Update apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts:1027-1064. Replace the local `planHasFeatured` derivation (line 1036-1038, `getPlanBySlug(planName)?.entitlements.includes(...)`) with `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured` (T-004). Align `shouldGrant`/`shouldRevoke` (lines 1040-1045) with the reconcile cron's status set: `shouldGrant` includes ACTIVE, TRIALING, and COMP (currently COMP is never checked here — add it); `shouldRevoke` includes CANCELLED, EXPIRED, and now PAUSED (currently PAUSED falls into the silent "skip" bucket per the comment at line 1034 — G-5 requires treating it as an explicit revoke, matching `loadEntitlements`/the cron, instead of relying on the 6h cron backstop to eventually clear it). Call the renamed `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner` (T-005) at line 1048.

Key files:
- apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts:1027-1064

Acceptance criteria:
- `shouldGrant` true for active/trialing/comp; `shouldRevoke` true for cancelled/expired/paused.
- `past_due`/other statuses remain a no-op (grace period, unchanged — only paused moves buckets).
- Uses T-004's resolver instead of the local `getPlanBySlug` check.
- Non-blocking try/catch wrapper preserved.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-023.

tasks.title: Rename and extend reconcile cron for addon-sourced, per-accommodation drift
tasks.description:
The T-006/SPEC-292 reconcile cron (apps/api/src/cron/jobs/featured-by-plan-reconcile.job.ts) only checks plan entitlements per OWNER (`resolveOwnerHasFeaturedListing`, lines 67-120, mirroring the plan-only path) — it does not see addon-sourced grants, so it cannot correct drift for the new per-accommodation source. Rename the file to featured-by-entitlement-reconcile.job.ts and extend its logic:

1. Keep the owner-wide plan pass using `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured` (T-004) instead of the local re-implementation (lines 102-111) — call `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner` (T-005) as before, which already excludes addon-protected accommodations on revoke.
2. ADD a per-accommodation pass: for accommodations NOT already covered by an owner-wide grant, check `resolveAccommodationHasActiveFeaturedAddon` (T-004) and call `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation` (T-005) when there is drift (addon says active but the column says false, or vice versa where the addon expired without the expiry hook firing — see T-016).
3. Update the cron registration name/description in apps/api/src/cron/schedules.manifest.ts:273 to reflect the new file/behavior; keep the existing 6h interval (cron-lag grace, per CLAUDE.md).

Key files:
- apps/api/src/cron/jobs/featured-by-plan-reconcile.job.ts -> featured-by-entitlement-reconcile.job.ts
- apps/api/src/cron/schedules.manifest.ts:273

Acceptance criteria:
- Cron corrects owner-wide plan drift as before (regression-safe).
- Cron ALSO corrects accommodation-scoped addon drift (both directions: grant missed, expiry-clear missed).
- Zero-drift fast path still makes no writes.
- Logs corrected-count per source (plan vs addon) for observability.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-029.

Update logging to distinguish plan vs addon corrections

Exhaustive addon-expiry-drift coverage deferred to T-029.

tasks.title: G-2: wire addon grant path to sync accommodation featuring
tasks.description:
In apps/api/src/services/addon-entitlement.service.ts, `applyAddonEntitlements` (lines ~148-173, after the QZPay `billing.entitlements.grant(...)` call succeeds): when the addon's `grantsEntitlement === EntitlementKey.FEATURED_LISTING` AND a `featured_listing_addon_grants` link row exists for this purchase (written by T-007 at confirm time), call `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation({ accommodationId, active: true })` (T-005) for the linked accommodation. Wrap in try/catch, soft-fail with a warn log (never block the entitlement grant itself) — mirrors the existing soft-fail pattern used by the T-005 (SPEC-292) billing call-sites.

Key files:
- apps/api/src/services/addon-entitlement.service.ts:148-173

Acceptance criteria:
- Purchasing a target-required, FEATURED_LISTING-granting addon flips `featuredByEntitlement=true` on the linked accommodation.
- Purchasing any other addon (no FEATURED_LISTING grant, or no link row) does not call the sync.
- A sync failure logs a warning and does not throw / does not block the entitlement grant.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-025.

subtasks.title: Detect FEATURED_LISTING grant + linked accommodation after applyAddonEntitlements success

tasks.title: G-2: wire addon expiry paths to clear accommodation featuring
tasks.description:
In apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts (existing daily cron, reused per OQ-5 — no new cron), wire the FEATURED_LISTING-clearing side-effect into BOTH places the addon's entitlement state changes:

1. Phase 1 expire path (~line 343-348, right after `AddonExpirationService.expireAddon` succeeds and `clearEntitlementCache` is called): if the expired purchase's addon granted FEATURED_LISTING and has a `featured_listing_addon_grants` link row, call `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation({ accommodationId, active: false })` (T-005) — which internally no-ops if the owner's plan still grants it (H-1 consequence a, mirrored for the expiry direction).
2. Grant-reconciliation retry block (~line 1593-1610, the `needsEntitlementSync` retry path): same wiring for the grant/re-grant direction (mirrors T-015's grant call) so a retried grant also re-syncs featuring.

Both call-sites are inside the existing transaction/critical-section structure of this cron — follow the file's established pattern for post-commit external-effect calls (the file already splits CLAIM-inside-tx / PROCESS-after-commit for a prior fix; the sync call is an internal DB write, not an external API call, so confirm whether it belongs inside or after the transaction and match the existing convention for similar internal writes in this file).

Key files:
- apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts (~343-348, ~1593-1610)

Acceptance criteria:
- An expired FEATURED_LISTING addon purchase clears `featuredByEntitlement` on its linked accommodation, UNLESS the owner's plan still grants it.
- A retried/re-applied grant re-syncs featuring to true.
- Both call-sites are soft-fail (a sync error does not abort the cron's other phases).
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-026.

subtasks.title: Wire expire path (~line 343-348) to clear featuring with plan-guard
Wire grant-reconciliation retry block (~line 1593-1610) to re-sync featuring
Confirm tx-boundary placement matches file's existing CLAIM/PROCESS convention

tasks.title: G-3: wire ISR/CDN revalidation into syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner
tasks.description:
Confirmed required per OQ-2 (search listing page is SSR+ISR with 24h Cloudflare edge cache; home page is SSG needing a redeploy). Replicate the exact pattern from apps/api/src/services/subscription-pause.service.ts:92-108 inside `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner` (T-005, packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.sync-featured-by-entitlement.ts): after the DB write succeeds and `updated.length > 0`, call `getRevalidationService()` (imported from `@repo/service-core`) and, if present, `revalidationService.scheduleRevalidationBatch({ events, reason })` fire-and-forget, where `events: EntityChangeData[]` is built from the `{ id, slug }` rows returned by `.returning()` (T-005 already added `slug`), each as `{ entityType: 'accommodation', slug }`. `reason` should be a descriptive string including the ownerId and active direction (e.g. \`featured-by-entitlement-owner: active=${active} ownerId=${ownerId}\`).

Key files:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.sync-featured-by-entitlement.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- Every successful bulk sync with `updated > 0` schedules an ISR revalidation batch for the affected accommodation slugs.
- Revalidation is fire-and-forget — never awaited in a way that blocks or fails the sync's return value.
- A missing/unavailable revalidation service (e.g. in tests without the binding) does not throw.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-027.

subtasks.title: Build events[] from returning() rows and call scheduleRevalidationBatch fire-and-forget

tasks.title: G-3: wire ISR/CDN revalidation into syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation
tasks.description:
Same pattern as T-017, applied to the single-row `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation` (T-005) so addon-driven featuring changes (G-2, T-015/T-016) are also reflected without waiting for TTL expiry. After a successful (non-no-op) write, call `getRevalidationService()?.scheduleRevalidationBatch({ events: [{ entityType: 'accommodation', slug }], reason })` fire-and-forget, using the `slug` from the function's `.returning()` result (T-005).

Key files:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.sync-featured-by-entitlement.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- A successful single-accommodation sync schedules a 1-event revalidation batch.
- A no-op (plan-guard skip, `updated: 0`) does NOT schedule revalidation.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-027.

subtasks.title: Wire revalidation into the single-accommodation sync path

tasks.title: G-6: owner self-service featured toggle — API route + entitlement gate
tasks.description:
SPEC-292 deliberately removed `isFeatured` from the owner-facing `AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema` (owner-leak closure, T-003 of that spec) — this task does NOT reopen that schema. Instead, add a NEW, narrowly-scoped protected endpoint whose write path is gated by a live entitlement check, not just field presence.

Add `PATCH /api/v1/protected/accommodations/:id/featured-toggle` (new route file under apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/protected/, e.g. `featured-toggle.ts`) with a minimal Zod body schema `{ isFeatured: boolean }`. In the route/service handler: resolve the accommodation, verify the requesting user owns it, then call a combined check — `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured({ ownerId })` OR `resolveAccommodationHasActiveFeaturedAddon({ accommodationId })` (both from T-004) — and reject with 403/`FORBIDDEN` if neither holds (the owner has no active FEATURED_LISTING entitlement for this accommodation, from either source). If entitled, write `isFeatured` directly via the accommodation model (no rotation/queue logic per OQ-4 — this is a pure visibility switch).

Key files:
- apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/protected/featured-toggle.ts (new)
- packages/schemas/src/accommodation/ (new minimal toggle schema, kept separate from `AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema`)

Acceptance criteria:
- An owner WITHOUT an active FEATURED_LISTING entitlement (plan or addon) for the target accommodation gets 403 when calling this endpoint.
- An owner WITH an active entitlement can toggle `isFeatured` true/false freely, uncapped (OQ-4).
- The general `AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema` remains unchanged — `isFeatured` is still stripped there (SPEC-292 regression guard, verify via existing test).
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-028.

subtasks.title: New minimal Zod schema for the toggle body
New protected route with ownership + entitlement gate (plan OR addon)

tasks.title: G-6: owner self-service featured toggle — web UI
tasks.description:
Add the owner-facing toggle control to the web owner accommodation editor at apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/mi-cuenta/propiedades/[id]/editar.astro (or its relevant client section under apps/web/src/components/host/editor/). Follow the `PlanEntitlementGate.client.tsx` precedent already used for gating other editor sections by plan entitlement — adapt or wrap it so the gate also considers an addon-derived entitlement (call the accommodation's current featured-entitlement state, e.g. via a small API read on the new T-019 route's underlying check, or a lightweight companion GET). Render the toggle only when the owner currently holds the entitlement (plan or addon) for THIS accommodation; otherwise render nothing or a subtle upsell (no rotation/queue UI per OQ-4 — keep it simple, a plain on/off switch). On toggle, call `PATCH .../featured-toggle` (T-019).

Key files:
- apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/mi-cuenta/propiedades/[id]/editar.astro
- apps/web/src/components/host/editor/ (new or extended component, vanilla CSS/CSS Modules per web styling convention — NOT Tailwind)

Acceptance criteria:
- Toggle is visible only for accommodations with an active FEATURED_LISTING entitlement (plan or addon).
- Toggling calls the T-019 endpoint and reflects the persisted state on success/failure.
- No slot/rotation UI — plain switch (OQ-4).
- Follows web app conventions: native HTML/small hook (no TanStack Form), vanilla CSS/CSS Modules, i18n for all labels.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-028 (component/interaction test) — see apps/web test conventions for the editor.

subtasks.title: Fetch/derive current entitlement-gated visibility for the toggle
Render toggle component (vanilla CSS, i18n) gated on entitlement
Wire toggle onChange to PATCH featured-toggle endpoint

tasks.title: Unit tests — resolver helpers (T-004)
tasks.description:
Test packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/featured-entitlement.resolver.ts using the hoisted-mock pattern already established in packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/sync-featured-by-plan.test.ts (vi.hoisted + vi.mock('@repo/db', ...)).

Cases:
- `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured`: true for active/trialing/comp subscription on a FEATURED_LISTING plan; false for paused/past_due/cancelled; false when no subscription.
- `resolveAccommodationHasActiveFeaturedAddon`: true when a `featured_listing_addon_grants` row joins to a purchase with status='active' and expiresAt in the future or null; false when the purchase is expired/cancelled or no link row exists.
- `getOwnerAccommodationIdsWithActiveFeaturedAddon`: returns exactly the protected id set for an owner with a mix of protected/unprotected accommodations; empty array when none.

Key files:
- packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/featured-entitlement.resolver.test.ts (new)

Acceptance criteria:
- All cases above pass.
- No live DB — pure mocked Drizzle per the established pattern.

Testing requirements:
- Run scoped: `pnpm vitest run packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/featured-entitlement.resolver.test.ts`.

tasks.title: Unit tests — sync primitives exclusion/guard (T-005)
tasks.description:
Extend/replace packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/sync-featured-by-plan.test.ts (rename to sync-featured-by-entitlement.test.ts alongside T-005's file rename), keeping its existing hoisted-mock scaffolding.

Cases:
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner(active: false)` excludes owner accommodations present in the addon-protected set (mock `getOwnerAccommodationIdsWithActiveFeaturedAddon` to return a non-empty set, assert the WHERE clause includes the exclusion).
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForOwner(active: true)` unconditional bulk update, unaffected by the addon-protected set (regression, existing SPEC-292 case).
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation(active: false)` is a no-op (`{ updated: 0 }`, no DB write) when `resolveOwnerPlanGrantsFeatured` mocks to true.
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation(active: false)` writes normally when the plan does NOT grant it.
- `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation(active: true)` always writes.
- Both functions' `.returning()` includes `slug`.

Key files:
- packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/sync-featured-by-entitlement.test.ts (renamed)

Acceptance criteria: all cases above pass; existing set/clear/empty/soft-deleted-exclusion cases from SPEC-292 still pass unmodified.

Testing requirements:
- Run scoped: `pnpm vitest run packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/sync-featured-by-entitlement.test.ts`.

tasks.title: Integration test — addon purchase to accommodation-scoped featuring, end-to-end
tasks.description:
Exercise the full OQ-3/G-1/G-2 chain in one integration test: create an addon checkout for `visibility-boost-7d` with a valid `accommodationId` (T-006) -> confirm the purchase (T-007, asserts a `featured_listing_addon_grants` row is written) -> assert `AddonEntitlementService.applyAddonEntitlements`'s post-grant hook (T-015) flips `featuredByEntitlement=true` ONLY on the targeted accommodation (not the owner's other accommodations, proving OQ-3 scoping — an owner-wide plan grant would flip all of them, an addon grant must flip exactly one).

Key files:
- apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.integration.test.ts (new, or extend an existing integration suite for addon purchase)

Acceptance criteria: the targeted accommodation's `featuredByEntitlement` becomes true; a second, unrelated accommodation owned by the same user stays false; the link row exists and correctly references both ids.

Testing requirements:
- Run scoped: `pnpm vitest run apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.integration.test.ts`.

tasks.title: Integration test — addon expiry plan-guard regression
tasks.description:
Proves H-1 consequence (a) and its mirror in T-016. Two scenarios against apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts's expire phase:

1. Owner has ONLY the addon-sourced grant (no FEATURED_LISTING plan) -> on addon expiry, `featuredByEntitlement` clears on the linked accommodation.
2. Owner has BOTH a FEATURED_LISTING plan AND the addon-sourced grant on the same accommodation -> on addon expiry, `featuredByEntitlement` stays true (plan-guard no-op in `syncFeaturedByEntitlementForAccommodation`, T-005/T-016).

Key files:
- apps/api/test/cron/addon-expiry.job.test.ts (extend)

Acceptance criteria: both scenarios assert the correct final `featuredByEntitlement` state; scenario 2 additionally asserts the sync primitive was called but resulted in `updated: 0` (no-op, not skipped entirely — the call still happens for auditability).

Testing requirements:
- Run scoped: `pnpm vitest run apps/api/test/cron/addon-expiry.job.test.ts`.

Also assert it is NOT called when `updated === 0` (no-op cases).

tasks.title: Docs: cross-reference and close-out notes
tasks.description:
Once all tasks above are green: leave a short cross-reference note documenting the SPEC-292 -> SPEC-309 relationship and the new architecture pieces for future readers. Suggested locations:

- A short note in this spec's directory or in packages/db/CLAUDE.md / packages/billing/CLAUDE.md (whichever already documents the featured-listing mechanism, if any) pointing to: the `featuredByEntitlement` rename (OQ-1), the new `featured_listing_addon_grants` link table and its purpose (OQ-3 scoping), and the reconcile cron rename.
- If CLAUDE.md's billing quick-reference section references `featuredByPlan` anywhere, update it to `featuredByEntitlement` and mention the addon-scoping mechanism briefly (1-2 lines), consistent with how the Promo code effect engine (SPEC-262) section is documented there.
- Note the SPEC-320 absorption is already recorded in the indices; no further action needed there.

Acceptance criteria: no dangling references to `featuredByPlan`/`syncFeaturedByPlan` remain in checked-in docs; the new mechanism is discoverable from CLAUDE.md or the nearest package CLAUDE.md.

Testing requirements:
- None (docs-only). Grep for `featuredByPlan` across `docs/` and `CLAUDE.md` files as a final check — zero hits expected outside historical/changelog references.
